rulings.Case Title: FIA CARD SERVICES, N.A. VS. BRIDGET V. BADASOW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 3, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF FIA CARD SERVICES, N.A.'s Notice Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Claim of Exemption is Granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to ernst@ernsthalperin.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: JANE DOE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Stay Of Arbitration Proceedigns To Allow Pltf To Pursue All Legal Remedies Avail In Superior Court
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 3, 2012, Line 4. PLAINTIFF JANE DOE Motion To Lift Stay Of Arbitration Proceedigns To Allow Plaintiff To Pursue All Legal Remedies Available In Superior Court. Granted in part. The litigation stay order issued by Judge Busch is vacated and the case will resume as an active civil case. The request to lift the arbitrator's stay order is denied. An application must be made to the arbitrator and plaintiff or defendant for that relief. Defendant's arguments regarding unclean hands, statute of limitations, tolling, and estoppel are not a basis to deny this motion. Defendant may raise those defenses later in the case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RYAN SMITH VS. TREGG SMITH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 03, 2012, Line 21. DEFENDANT TREGG SMITH'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled. The cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is adequately pled. Per Code of Civil Procedure section 452, the court can reasonably infer that a person being pushed out of a 5-story window will experience severe emotional distress. rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 3, 2012, Line 10. DEFENDANT KIRANSINH THAKOR, SHREE JALARAM LODGING LP DBA BUDGET INN, BALWANTSINH THAKOR, THAKOR, LATABAN MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Plaintiffs have pled sufficient facts to support their request for punitive damages. rulings.Case Title: SUZMAN DESIGN ASSOCIATES, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DUSTIN MOORE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, May 03, 2012, Line 22. PLAINTIFF SUZMAN DESIGN ASSOCIATES, INC.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED ANSWER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the 3rd, 4th, 5th 6th, 9th, 10th, 11th, 13th and 14th affirmative defenses and sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the 2nd, 7th and 8th affirmative defenses. The allegations of the 3rd, 4th, 5th 6th, 9th, 10th, 11th, 13th and 14th affirmative defenses follow the form and practice in this and other California courts and sustaining the demurrer as to any of those defenses would not advance the case. The allegations of the 2nd, 7th and 8th affirmative defenses do not constitute new matter, and defendants are given leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege true affirmative defenses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
